title: Functions should avoid containing too many return statements
description: Having too many return statements in a function increases the function’s essential complexity because the flow of execution is broken each time a return statement is encountered. This makes it harder to read and understand the logic of the function. The way of counting the return statements is aligned with the way we compute Cognitive Complexity.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Functions should avoid containing too many return statements. Consider refactoring to reduce complexity.
